content.claim: Primary school student enrollment is decreasing while secondary and tertiary enrollment increased in 2024/2025, diverging from broader demographic decline that has continued since 1981 and produced irregular age pyramids with major differences in generation sizes, complicating cohort-based capacity planning for selection-point restructuring.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: Year-on-year reversal in primary trend may be temporary fluctuation rather than durable shift
Irregular age pyramid prevents simple linear forecasting
Does not specify regional variation in demographic patterns
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
